This module deals with interpreting the parse tree as Python
would have done, in the compiler.
For now this only covers parse tree to value conversion of
compile-time values.
absolute_import
CompileErrorc
EmptyScopec
self
namer
A/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/Cython/Compiler/Interpreter.py
lookup
EmptyScope.lookupN)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__r
    Tries to interpret a list of compile time option nodes.
    The result will be a tuple (optlist, optdict) but where
    all expression nodes have been interpreted. The result is
    in the form of tuples (value, pos).
    optlist is a list of nodes, while optdict is a DictNode (the
    result optdict is a dict)
    If type_env is set, all type nodes will be analysed and the resulting
    type set. Otherwise only interpretateable ExprNodes
    are allowed, other nodes raises errors.
    A CompileError will be raised if there are problems.
    c
Invalid type.z
Type not allowed here.r
analyse_as_typer
version_info
isinstanceZ
StringNodeZ
unicode_valueZ
compile_time_value
empty_scope)
node
type)
	type_args
type_envr
	interpret'
z0interpret_compiletime_options.<locals>.interpretc
<listcomp>8
z1interpret_compiletime_options.<locals>.<listcomp>N)
	enumerater
DictNodeZ
key_value_pairs
value)
optlistZ
optdictr
new_optdict
itemZ
new_keyZ
dummyr
interpret_compiletime_options
__doc__Z

NodesZ	ExprNodesZ

objectr
<module>
